title: We are leaving Monday

posts.author: Shu (Shu Guo)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have moved to Monday 1.5 years ago, after 13 years of experience using other platforms.
Apparently we’ve done a lot of research and really liked lots of functions that Monday provides.
We have found that, however, some key functions important to us are either missing don’t work well enough:

  • The gantt chart function is a bit clunky. For example, to set up dependency, you can not set it up by dragging lines between items in the chart, like many other gantt chart tools do, but have to go back to table view and search for the proper item - making straightforward thing difficult and time consuming.
  • The time tracking tool is hopeless. You cannot export all time record across all boards with details. If you need details (ie start and finish time, etc) you have to go to specific board, and the formate it exported is not further editable. How can this most simple but important function missing?
  • The speed is getting slower and slower.

I wonder if I have something wrong or missing. But if I’m correct I have to regretfully leave and switch to something simpler but much quicker like Trello + a professional gantt chart tool.

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Shu! Thank you for posting - we are so sorry to hear that you are leaving our platform.

We really appreciate your feedback and we want to make sure we help you get the most out of your account - whether you stay with us for many more years or only use it for a few more days.

Regarding the Gantt chart problems, you should be able to drag the items around to change the dependencies as you please!
You can easily shift dependencies in the Gantt chart by clicking a timeline bar and dragging it the exact number of days it has been delayed, as mentioned in both “The Gantt Chart View and Widget” under “Show dependencies”, and in the “Get started with monday projects” article under “Use the Gantt Chart to stay on top of deadlines”.

Can you take a look at those instructions and confirm if you are still experiencing difficulty with this?

We hear you about the issues exporting the Time Tracking columns. Have you tried using two Date Columns or a Number Column instead if you are looking to export it often?

Regarding the speed, we are so sorry to hear you have been running into this problem! Do you use Google Chrome? Or are you using a different browser or the monday desktop app?

Let us know your thoughts - we’d love to help fix this and keep you as part of our community, if we can!

posts.author: Shu (Shu Guo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for your email Charlotte. Please see my response in red.

| Charlotte Kirkham CharlotteK monday.com Team Member
August 30 |

  • | - |

Hey Shu! Thank you for posting - we are so sorry to hear that you are leaving our platform.

We really appreciate your feedback and we want to make sure we help you get the most out of your account - whether you stay with us for many more years or only use it for a few more days.

Regarding the Gantt chart problems, you should be able to drag the items around to change the dependencies as you please!
You can easily shift dependencies in the Gantt chart by clicking a timeline bar and dragging it the exact number of days it has been delayed, as mentioned in both “The Gantt Chart View and Widget” under “Show dependencies”, and in the “Get started with monday projects” article under “Use the Gantt Chart to stay on top of deadlines”.

We understand the basics of dependencies as described in both articles. My complaint on the post was that it can only be set in the main table under the dependency column or pop-out window and via a manual selection of the items. It is ok if it is designed in this way. But it would be better if it could be done in a more straightforward way like dragging from the diagram directly to create dependencies.

Can you take a look at those instructions and confirm if you are still experiencing difficulty with this?

We hear you about the issues exporting the Time Tracking columns. Have you tried using two Date Columns or a Number Column instead if you are looking to export it often?

Can you explain a bit more about how to do time records by setting up date and number columns? currently, we are using a paid app “Tracket” which is still not perfect as it cannot record start and finish time of each record, even though it can export every record across all boards, which is missing from the office time record function of Monday.

Regarding the speed, we are so sorry to hear you have been running into this problem! Do you use Google Chrome? Or are you using a different browser or the monday desktop app?

Yes we use Chrome ver. 116.0.5845.110

posts.author: RomanF (Roman Fianta)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Shu,

this is Roman, Product Manager at Appfire.

We have just introduced an app that could fulfill your needs as it records and exports start and finish time of every time entry - 7pace Timetracker. It’s in a ‘public preview’ stage and thus it’s free for now, so maybe you can give it a shot and let us know if it helps in your case?

I’ll be happy to walk you through the app if needed, just book my time in case you’re interested.

Best regards,
Roman
